title: vvp: Improve partial vector write handling
body:
There are a few instructions that allow for partial writes to vectors. They all
have similar but not identical implementations.

When doing a partial write it is possible that the assigned value is either
partially or fully out-of-bounds. For partial out-of-bounds writes the value
needs to be truncated so that it is in bounds. For fully out-of-bounds writes
the assignment should be skipped.

There are two small problems with regards to the handling of out-of-bounds writes.

1) Non-blocking and non-blocking event controlled writes mix-up the target signal
width and value width, which can misclassify a fully out-of-bounds write on the
low side as a partial out-of-bounds write, which triggers an assert.

2) Blocking writes to arrays do not handle partial or fully out-of-bounds writes.
Trying to do so will trigger an assert. 

Add a helper function that will handle the checking and resizing that is
required for out-of-bounds writes. This ensures consistent and correct
behavior between all the functions.

In addition to this 2-state arrays do not support partial writes at all. Trying
to do so will trigger an assert. Add support for this by implementing them for
2-state arrays the same way as for 4-state arrays.
